label: Azure SQL Database
description: Connect to a Microsoft-managed Azure SQL Database instance using host, port, credentials, and encrypted TLS.

form.title: Connect Azure SQL Database
form.subtitle: Provide your Azure SQL server hostname, database name, and credentials to establish an encrypted connection.

methods.label: SQL Authentication
methods.description: Connect with a SQL login username and password. The simplest path — paste your server hostname, database name, and credentials directly from the Azure Portal.
methods.how_to:
## SQL Authentication

Use a SQL login (username + password) to connect. This works for both Azure SQL Database server admins and lower-privilege SQL users.

### Find your connection details

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) and navigate to **SQL databases**.
2. Click your database, then select **Overview** in the left panel.
3. Copy the **Server name** — it looks like `yourserver.database.windows.net`.
4. Note the **Database name** shown on the same page.

### Get or create a SQL user

5. If you want to use the server admin, you set the admin login when you created the server. Retrieve it under **Settings → Properties** (the login name, not password).
6. To create a lower-privilege user, connect with an admin tool (e.g. Azure Data Studio or SSMS) and run:
   ```sql
   CREATE USER anton WITH PASSWORD = 'StrongPass123!';
   GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA::dbo TO anton;
   ```

### Firewall rules

7. In the Azure Portal, go to your SQL server → **Security → Networking**.
8. Under **Firewall rules**, add the IP address of the machine running Anton, or enable **Allow Azure services** if Anton runs in Azure.

### Fill in the form

9. Paste the server hostname, port (default `1433`), database name, username, and password below. Encryption is always enabled — Azure SQL requires it.

### Notes
- Passwords must meet Azure complexity requirements.
- The server admin cannot be used to access `master` database operations by default on shared tiers.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Server hostname
fields.description: Found in Azure Portal → SQL databases → Overview → Server name.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Azure SQL uses port 1433 by default. Change only if your server uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database name
fields.description: The specific database to connect to, as shown in Azure Portal → SQL databases.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: The SQL login username. Use the server admin login or a dedicated SQL user you created.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the SQL login. Stored encrypted locally and never sent to Anton servers.

fields.name: encrypt
fields.label: Require encryption (TLS)
fields.description: Azure SQL always requires encrypted connections. Keep this enabled.

fields.name: trust_server_certificate
fields.label: Trust server certificate
fields.description: Skip server certificate validation. Not recommended for production — leave off to validate Azure's TLS certificate.

methods.label: Azure Active Directory — Password
methods.description: Authenticate with an Azure AD (Entra ID) user principal and password. Required when your Azure SQL server enforces Azure AD-only authentication.
methods.how_to:
## Azure Active Directory Password Authentication

Use this method when your Azure SQL server is configured with **Azure AD-only authentication** (SQL logins are disabled) or when you want to authenticate as an Entra ID user.

### Prerequisites

1. Your Azure SQL server must have **Azure Active Directory admin** set. In the Azure Portal, go to your SQL server → **Settings → Azure Active Directory** and confirm an AAD admin is configured.
2. The user you want to connect as must exist as an **external user** (Azure AD user) in the database. Connect as the AAD admin and run:
   ```sql
   CREATE USER [user@yourdomain.com] FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER;
   GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA::dbo TO [user@yourdomain.com];
   ```

### Find your connection details

3. Open **SQL databases → Overview** and copy the **Server name** (`yourserver.database.windows.net`).
4. Note the **Database name**.

### Firewall

5. Under your SQL server → **Security → Networking**, ensure your machine's IP is allowed.

### Fill in the form

6. Enter the server hostname, port (`1433`), database, your Azure AD email as the username, and your Azure AD password below.

### Notes
- Multi-factor authentication (MFA) accounts cannot use password flow. Use a service principal or a non-MFA account.
- Guest accounts from external tenants may not be supported.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Server hostname
fields.description: Found in Azure Portal → SQL databases → Overview → Server name.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Azure SQL uses port 1433 by default.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database name
fields.description: The specific database to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Azure AD username
fields.description: Your Azure AD (Entra ID) email address — must exist as an external user in the target database.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Azure AD password
fields.description: Your Azure AD account password. MFA-enabled accounts are not supported with this method.

fields.name: encrypt
fields.label: Require encryption (TLS)
fields.description: Azure SQL always requires encrypted connections. Keep this enabled.

methods.label: Azure AD Service Principal
methods.description: Authenticate as an Azure AD app registration (client ID + secret). Best for automated or headless access without a user account.
methods.how_to:
## Azure AD Service Principal Authentication

Use an Entra ID (Azure AD) app registration to authenticate Anton as a non-interactive service identity. Ideal for automation and CI/CD scenarios.

### Register an app in Entra ID

1. Open the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com) → **Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration**.
2. Give it a name (e.g. `Anton SQL`), leave the redirect URI blank, and click **Register**.
3. Copy the **Application (client) ID** and **Directory (tenant) ID** from the Overview page.
4. Go to **Certificates & secrets → New client secret**. Add a description, set an expiry, and click **Add**.
5. Copy the **Value** immediately — it won't be shown again.

### Grant database access

6. Connect to your Azure SQL server as an AAD admin and run:
   ```sql
   CREATE USER [Anton SQL] FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER;
   GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA::dbo TO [Anton SQL];
   ```
   Replace `Anton SQL` with your app registration's display name exactly.

### Ensure your SQL server allows AAD auth

7. Azure Portal → your SQL server → **Settings → Azure Active Directory** — confirm an AAD admin is set.

### Firewall

8. Add your machine's IP under the SQL server → **Security → Networking → Firewall rules**.

### Notes
- Client secrets expire. Rotate before expiry and update the secret in Anton.
- The tenant ID is the GUID on the Entra ID Overview page.

fields.name: client_secret

fields.description: The secret value you copied when creating the client secret. Shown only once at creation time.

fields.description: Azure SQL always requires encrypted connections. Keep this enabled.
